source_metadata.title_raw: Jay Goldstone - A-19-064a - October 10, 2019 - Los Angeles

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 10, 2019

Jay Goldstone
Managing Director
MUFG Union Bank, N.A.
445 S. Figueroa St., Mail Code G05-268
Los Angeles, CA 90071

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-064

Dear Mr. Goldstone:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the Act).¹ Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate.

QUESTION

Is MUFG required to submit Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700) as a consultant subject to full disclosure as directed by CalHR considering the scope of the contract work you perform for CalHR?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Based on the facts presented, the primary manager of the investment fund must file a Form 700 as a consultant subject to full disclosure as directed by CalHR.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You are the managing director of MUFG Union Bank, N.A. (MUFG or you). On March 12, 2019, you received a letter from the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) stating that MUFG is required by law, regulation, and CalHR's conflict of interest code to file a Form 700 Statement of Economic Interests with the FPPC ("Form 700").

MUFG has contracted with CalHR to provide a demand deposit account that pays an agreed upon interest rate (the Account). The Account is part of a short-term investment fund cash

_______________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-064
Page No. 2

investment option in which State employees may elect to invest under CalHR's Savings Plus
Program. State employee earnings in the Savings Plus Program are a function of the interest rate
applied to the balances and not based upon any other specific performance.

The term of MUFG's contract is a five-year period beginning March 1, 2017, with the
option to extend the Agreement up to two additional one-year periods. (Contract at Exhibit A, p. 2.)
The contract states in relevant part:

All parties understand that this Agreement and its exhibits are established to cover
certain terms and conditions regarding Savings Plus Program assets invested by the
Department in the Contractors [FDIC-insured] deposit savings account product (the
Account).

(Id. at Exhibit A, p. 1.)

WHEREAS, the Department desires to utilize the Account as an authorized investment
strategy in the Plan's Short-Term Investment Fund (STIF)-Cash investment option, in which
State Employees (hereinafter referred to as "Participants") may elect to invest[.]

WHEREAS, the Department may utilize the Account as an authorized investment
strategy in various other investment options, in which Participants may elect to invest[.]

(Id. at Exhibit A, p. 2.)

ANALYSIS

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial
interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Consequently, every agency
must adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.)

A conflict of interest code must be formulated at the most decentralized level possible
(Section 87301) 2; enumerate the positions within the agency, including consultant positions, that
involve making or participation in the making of decisions which may have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on any financial interest (Section 87302(a)); and set forth the
specific types of investments, business positions, real property interests, or sources of income which
are reportable for each enumerated position (ibid).

Government Code Section 87200 further provides that, among other people, "other public
officials who manage public investments" must file statements of economic interests. Known as
statutory filers, public officials subject to Section 87200 are subject to full disclosure under the Act.
(See Sections 87206 through 87209.)

2 As the determination of whether an individual is a public official subject to disclosure is best made by the
employing agency, we generally defer to the agency's determination unless there is reason to suggest that the
determination is incorrect.

File No. A-19-064
Page No. 3

A. Public Official.

The term "public official" includes "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency." (Section 82048 [emphasis added]; and Regulation 18700.) The issue here is whether you, as contracted investment manager for CalHR, are a "consultant" under the Act. Regulation 18700.3(a)(2) defines a "consultant" to include an individual who, pursuant to a contract with an agency, does the following:

Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity participates in making a governmental decision as defined in Regulation 18704(a) and (b) or performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the agency that would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position specified in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code under Section 87302.

The existence of an ongoing relationship between the contractor and the public agency is significant. We have previously found that a contractor serves in a staff capacity when the contract calls for work to be performed "over more than one year" on "high level" projects (Ennis Advice Letter, No. A-15-006; see also Ferber Advice Letter, No. A-98-118). We have further advised that a contractor does not act in a staff capacity where the work is to be performed on one project or a limited number of projects over a limited period of time (Sanchez Advice Letter, No. A-97-438); where the relationship between the contractor and the agency would last only 12-16 months with no ongoing relationship contemplated (Harris Advice Letter, No. A-02-239); and where, under a multi-year contract, the contractor would perform only on a sporadic basis. (Maze Advice Letter, No. I-95-296; Parry Advice Letter, No. I-95-064.)

Under the facts provided, MUFG is not providing CalHR with temporary or limited services. Rather, the contractual relationship is ongoing. The existing contract is for a five-year term beginning March 1, 2017, with the option to extend the Agreement up to two additional one-year periods.

In addition to serving in a staff capacity, a "consultant" must "participate in making a governmental decision, which includes a public official's authorization or direction of any action by an agency." (See Regulation 18704(a).) "Participating in a decision" occurs when the official "provides information, an opinion, or a recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision without significant intervening substantive review." (Regulation 18704(b).) "Significant intervening substantive review" has been interpreted to require more than the mere review of the recommendations by superiors, but rather the independent checking of the results without solely relying on the data of the official. (Greenwold Advice Letter, No. I-90-349.)

In this case, CalHR utilizes MUFG's short-term investment fund – cash investment option for State employee investments in the Savings Plus Program. (Contract at Exhibit A, p. 2.) As a contractor providing an investment fund option for State employee investors in CalHR's Savings Plus Program, MUFG participates in making a governmental decision under Regulation 18700.3(a)(2). Accordingly, we find that the MUFG's primary manager of the investment fund is a consultant and public official under the Act.

File No. A-19-064
Page No. 4

B.     Managing a Public Investment.

     Under Regulation 18700.3(b)(1), "Others public officials who manage public investments" is defined to include:

     (B) High-level officers and employees of public agencies who exercise primary responsibility for the management of public investments such as chief or principal investment officers or chief financial managers....

     (C) Individuals who, pursuant to a contract with a state or local government agency, perform the same or substantially all the same functions that would otherwise be performed by the public officials described in subdivision (b)(1)(B).

     "Public investments" is defined as "the investment of public moneys in real estate, securities, or other economic interests for the production of revenue or other financial return." (Regulation 18700.3(c).) Moreover, "public money is defined as "all money belonging to, received by, or held by, the state...." (Regulation 18700(d).)

     In this case, the money held in the Savings Plus Programs is received by and held by the State and MUFG has been contracted to provide the ongoing and primary management of the investment of these public moneys. Accordingly, MUFG's primary manager of the investment fund is also a public official who manages a public investment and a statutory filer under Section 87200 subject to full disclosure. Based on the facts provided, the primary manager of the investment fund must file Statements of Economic Interests as directed by CalHR.

     If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                        Sincerely,

                                                        Dave Bainbridge
                                                        General Counsel

                                        By:    John M. Feser Jr.
                                               Senior Counsel, Legal Division

JMF:aja

sections.question:
Is MUFG Union Bank, N.A., its employees, or Jay Goldstone, its primary contact on a 
contract with CalHR involving an investment fund in the CalHR Savings Plus Program (collectively 
MUFG), required to submit Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700) as a consultant subject to 
full disclosure as directed by CalHR considering the scope of the co.ntract work it performs for 
CalHR?
sections.conclusion:
No. Based on the facts presented, MUFG is not a consultant subject to full disclosure and is 
not required to file a Form 700. 
1 This letter supersedes A-19-064, based upon additional information received from the requester and 
consultation with CalHR.
sections.facts:
A. 
The Contract. 
a  
CalHR and MUFG entered into a five-year contract commencing March 1, 2017 and ending 
on February 28, 2022 (the Contract). Under the Contract, MUFG provides CalHR a FDIC insured 
demand savings deposit account (the Account) that pays a specified amount of interest based on 
terms in the Contract. The Account is one of the authorized short-term investment fund, cash 
investment options offered to CalHR employees as part of CalHR's Savings Plus Program (the 
Program). 
In addition to the Contract with MUFG, and as part of the Contract terms, CalHR also 
entered into a contract with Nationwide Retirement Solutions, a Third Party Administrator (TPA), 
and JP Morgan Chase Bank, Trustee/Custodian and Securities Lending Agent (the Trustee). The 
TPA provides all of the record keeping for the Account and the Trustee provides the trust services. 
The terms of this contract (the TPA/Trustee Contract) require that MUFG comply with all 
directions provided by the TP A and the Trustee. 
The TP A manages a "clearing" account at another banking institution. The TP A receives the 
deposits from the CalHR employees who opt to participate in the Program, and issues withdrawals 
of funds upon request by the employees. On a periodic basis, the TPA makes deposits into, and 
withdrawals from, the Account. 
Under TPA/Trustee Contract, MUFG manages and invests funds in the Account, but the 
funds are not subject to market or investment risk because it is similar to a bank savings account. 
The TPA/Trustee Contract mandates that MUFG implement the following ministerial functions: (1) 
operate the Account in a daily unit accounting environment; (2) permit additions and withdrawals 
of assets from the Account; (3) pay a pre-determined interest rate based on the methodology 
specified in the contract; (4) reconcile the Account with the Trustee; and (5) collateralize Program 
participants' assets, and provide the required reports to CalHR. 
In addition, the TPA/Trustee Contract specifies that CalHR will determine the short-term 
investment fund cash allocations in the best interest of Program participants and their beneficiaries. 
CalHR retains sole discretion for the determination of the specific allocation of participant funds 
into each short-term investment fund cash member institution. CalHR unilaterally determines the 
amount of funds deposited in the Account. Consequently, no MUFG employee performs the 
function of a chief or principal investment officer or financial manager of the funds in the Account. 
Based on the terms of the Contract, the Account earnings are set by the pre-determined 
interest rate and are not based on any discretionary investments or performance strategies by 
MUFG. Other than tracking the balance of funds, the deposits in the Account are not segregated and 
specifically tracked for investment or return purposes. 
· 


B. 
Jay Goldstone's Duties and Responsibilities Under the Contract. 
a  
Jay Goldstone is a Global Relationship Manager (GRM) for MUFG with a focus on public 
sector accounts such as cities, counties, states and special districts. He is a marketing professional 
whose responsibilities include sales, obtaining new business and clients and expanding existing 
business, relationship building, and customer service. He also markets products and services 
provided by his product partners. His primary focus is on products such as letters of credit, direct 
purchases of securities, and bank loans. 
Mr. Goldstone prepared the bid in response to the original CalHR Request for Proposal and 
negotiated the terms of, and amendments to, the Contract. He worked with the transaction banking 
partner whose responsibility includes treasury products, such as the deposit for the Ac.count. He 
also works with several MUFG personnel to prepare the annual presentation 'regarding the Account, 
including collateral and risk management. He is not involved in the day-to-day management, 
administration or servicing of the Account and does not (1) manage any of the funds in the 
Account; (2) direct the investment of the funds in the Account; (3) formulate or approve investment 
policies for the Account; ( 4) approve or establish guidelines for asset allocations for the Account, 
(5) approve investment transactions for the Account; (6) make any discretionary decisions or take 
any discretionary actions regarding investments of funds in the Account; or (7) provide information, 
opinions or recommendations to CalHR for the purpose of affecting a decision by the agency 
regarding the use of funds in the Account or who can make deposits to the Account. 
C. 
Other MUFG Duties and Responsibilities Under the Contract. 
The duties and responsibilities of the Treasury Relationship Manager (TRM) for MUFG are 
similar to the GRM, including sales, relationship building and customer support. The TRM's 
primary focus, however, is treasury products such as deposits. The TRM assigned to the Contract 
worked with Mr. Goldstone regarding the deposit for the Account. Similar to Mr. Goldstone, the 
TRM is not involved in the day-to-day management, administration or servicing of the Account and 
is not involved in the management or investment of the funds in the Account. 
Employees in the Collateral Group ensure that adequate collateral is set aside to meet 
MUFG's obligations under the contract. Pursuant to instructions provided by the TPA, this amount 
varies from month to month. Periodic collateral reports are provided by these employees to CalHR.
sections.analysis:
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial 
interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Consequently, every agency 
must adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.) 
r 


a  
A conflict of interest code must be formulated at the most decentralized level possible 
(Section 87301) 3; enumerate the positions within the agency, including consultant positions, that 
involve making or participation in the making of decisions which may have a reasonably 
foreseeable material financial effect on any financial interest (Section 87302(a)); and set forth the 
specific types of investments, business positions; real property interests, or sources of income which 
are reportable for each enumerated position (ibid). 
Government Code Section 87200 further provides that, among other people, "other public 
officials who manage public• investments" must file statements of economic interests. Known as 
statutory filers, public officials subject to Section 87200 are subject to full disclosure under the Act. 
(See Sections 87206 through 87209.) 
Section 87200 is applicable to "other public officials who manage public investments." The 
term "public official" includes "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local 
government agency." (Section 82048 [emphasis added]; Regulation 18700.) The issue here is 
whether a contracted investment manag~r for CalHR, is a "consultant" under the Act. 
Regulation 18700.3(a)(2) defines a "consultant" to include an individual who, pursuanrto a 
contract with an agency, does the following: 
Serves in a staff capacity with the agency and in that capacity participates in 
making a governmental decision as defined in Regulation 18704(a) and (b) or 
performs the same or substantially all the same duties for the agency that 
would otherwise be performed by an individual holding a position specified 
in the agency's Conflict oflnterest Code under Section 87302.4 
In addition to serving in a staff capacity, a "consultant" must "participate in making a 
governmental decision, which includes a public official's authorization or direction of any action by 
an agency." (See Regulation 18704(a).) "Participating in a decision" occurs when the official 
"provides information, an opinion, or a recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision 
without significant intervening substantive review." (Regulation 18704(b).) "Significant intervening 
substantive review" has been interpreted to require more than the mere review of the 
recommendations by superiors, but rather the independent checking of the results without solely 
relying on the data of the official. (Greenwald Advice Letter, No. I-90-349.) 
Under Regulation 18700.3(b)(l), "other public officials who manage public 
investments" include: 
(B) High-level officers and employees of public agencies who exercise 
primary responsibility for the management of public investments such as 
chief or principal investment officers or chief financial managers .... 
3 As the determination of whether an individual is a public official subject to disclosure is best made by the 
employing agency, we generally defer to the agency's determination unless there is reason to suggest that the 
determination is incorrect. 
4 Under Regulation 18700(a), individuals who make certain governmental decisions pursuant to a contract with 
a state or local agency are also consultants under the Act. However, based on the facts provided, MUFG and its 
employees do not make any of the decisions specified in Regulation l8700(a). 


a  
(C) Individuals who, pursuant to a contract with a state or local government 
agency, perform the same or substantially all the same functions that would 
otherwise be performed by the public officials described in subdivision 
(b )(1 )(B). 
Regulation 18700.3 also sets forth the following definitions: 
( c) "Public investments" means the investment of public moneys in real 
estate, securities, or other economic interests for the production of revenue or 
other financial return. (Regulation 18700.3(c).) 
(d) "Public moneys" means all money belonging to, received by, or held by, 
the state .... 
( e) "Management of public investments" means the following nonministerial 
functions: directing the investment of public moneys; formulating or 
approving investment policies; approving or establishing guidelines for asset 
allocations; or approving investment transactions. 
In this case, the pertinent issues are whether MUFG or its employees serve in a staff 
capacity and participate in governmental decisions. Based on the facts provided, the Contract 
requires MUFG to provide CalHR with the FDIC insured Account that pays interest established by 
terms set forth in the Contract. MUFG may manage and invest its earnings from the Account like a 
bank does with funds deposited into a savings account, but that management and investment does 
not affect the Account, the State, the State employee investors, or MUFG's earnings under the 
Contract. MUFG's Account earnings are set by the pre-determined interest rate and are not based 
on any discretionary investments or performance strategies by MUFG. 
Accordingly, MUFG and its employees are not serving in a staff capacity and participating 
in governmental decisions by performing its duties under the contract. The facts do not indicate that 
MUFG or its employees are managing public investments. MUFG and its employees do not act as 
the chief or principal investment officer or financial manager of the funds in the Account. Based on 
the facts provided, MUFG and its employees are not consultants or public officials under the Act 
and not required file a Form 700 Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is MUFG Union Bank, N.A., its employees, or Jay Goldstone, its primary contact on a 
contract with CalHR involving an investment fund in the CalHR Savings Plus Program (collectively 
MUFG), required to submit Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700) as a consultant subject to 
full disclosure as directed by CalHR considering the scope of the co.ntract work it performs for 
CalHR?

CONCLUSION: No. Based on the facts presented, MUFG is not a consultant subject to full disclosure and is 
not required to file a Form 700. 
1 This letter supersedes A-19-064, based upon additional information received from the requester and 
consultation with CalHR.
